key_insight: The paper defines a “lattice gene” and “lattice genome” to compactly encode local crystalline structure and spatially map microstructural heterogeneity from EBSD diffraction patterns.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical way to quantify grain-scale and intragranular structure in advanced alloys, supporting microstructure-property control in manufacturing and heat treatment.
method: Encode Kikuchi diffraction patterns with a variational autoencoder to create low-dimensional lattice genes.
Assemble spatially resolved lattice genomes from EBSD maps over representative areas.
Use latent-space distance, angle, and spread metrics for visualization, segmentation, and heterogeneity quantification.
evidence_signal: The approach is validated on EBSD microstructures of additively manufactured and wrought Ni-base superalloys in both as-built and recrystallized states.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and representativeness of EBSD maps, and latent metrics may require careful calibration to remain physically meaningful across materials and conditions.
paper_title: Lattice genome: representation and analysis of heterogeneous crystalline microstructures
